name: Daganzo de Arriba y El Casar — circular by the Campiña de Guadalajara
name_original: Daganzo de Arriba y El Casar — circular por la Campiña de Guadalajara
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Azuqueca De Henares benefit from the region's diverse terrain, situated within the Henares River basin and the Campiña de Guadalajara. The landscape features open fields and gentle hills, providing varied gradients for cyclists. Many routes follow the Henares River, offering riverside scenery. The area provides a network of road cycling routes suitable for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Azuqueca De Henares The most popular road cycling route is Daganzo de Arriba y El Casar — circular by the Campiña de Guadalajara, a 62.3 miles (100.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 17 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the open landscapes of the Campiña de Guadalajara. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Matarrubia – Parish Church of San Miguel loop from Azuqueca, a moderate 57.8 miles (93.1 km) path. This route offers a substantial ride through the surrounding countryside, featuring varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Albolleque–Chiloeches Climb – Pioz Castle loop from Azuqueca, a 49.0 miles (78.9 km) trail leading through the rural areas towards Pioz Castle, often completed in about 3 hours 32 minutes. Road cycling around Azuqueca De Henares is defined by the Henares River, open fields, and gentle hills of the Campiña de Guadalajara. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Azuqueca De Henares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Azuqueca De Henares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Azuqueca De Henares?
A: Azuqueca De Henares offers a good selection of road cycling routes, with over 20 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging excursions.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Azuqueca De Henares?
A: The region's diverse terrain, situated within the Henares River basin and the Campiña de Guadalajara, means you'll encounter a mix of open fields and gentle hills. Many routes follow the Henares River, offering picturesque riverside scenery and unique natural terraces.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Azuqueca De Henares?
A: Yes, Azuqueca De Henares has several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31947360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of San Miguel loop from Azuqueca&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 33.4 km ride with minimal elevation gain, ideal for a relaxed outing.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Experienced cyclists can find more demanding routes with significant distances and elevation gains. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016390164/daganzo-de-arriba-y-el-casar-circular-by-the-campina-de-guadalajara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daganzo de Arriba y El Casar — circular by the Campiña de Guadalajara&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 100.3 km route with over 800 meters of climbing, offering a substantial challenge.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Azuqueca De Henares are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10425875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarrubia – Parish Church of San Miguel loop from Azuqueca&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, providing a 93.1 km loop through the countryside.

Q: What natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: You can enjoy the natural beauty of the Henares River and its basin. The area is also home to the Ornithological Reserve of Azuqueca de Henares, a vital resting place for migratory birds. Additionally, you might pass by highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6115319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cerros Forest Park&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3615421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of Joy&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or points of interest accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, several routes pass by historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6935623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albolleque–Chiloeches Climb – Pioz Castle loop from Azuqueca&lt;/a&gt; leads towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2409049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pioz Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2393087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3405880" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace of the Infantado&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Azuqueca De Henares?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Azuqueca De Henares, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters are typically cooler but still rideable on clear days.

Q: Is parking available near the road cycling routes?
A: Azuqueca De Henares, being a well-connected town, offers various parking options. Many routes start from or pass through areas where public parking is available, especially near urban parks or town centers, making it convenient to access the trails.

Q: How can I access the road cycling routes using public transport?
A: Azuqueca De Henares is well-served by public transport, including train and bus services, which can facilitate access to the starting points of many routes. It's advisable to check local transport schedules and bicycle carriage policies beforehand.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Azuqueca De Henares?
A: The road cycling routes in Azuqueca De Henares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views along the Henares River, and the network of routes that cater to different ability levels.
tips: The natural environment is one of the attractions of the town due to the cereal steppe, the riverside forest and the gentle hills and slopes. You can see a lot of birds in the area (great bustard, montagu's harrier, lesser kestrel, black vulture).

Plaza de la Constitución: A great place to stroll and soak up the local atmosphere.
Additionally, Algete has a rich history and local traditions that you can explore.
